jarint.h

00025 
00026 /*=========================================================================
00027  * SYSTEM:    KVM
00028  * SUBSYSTEM: JAR file reader.
00029  * FILE:      jarint.h
00030  * OVERVIEW:  Internal declarations for JAR file reader. This file should
00031  *            be included only by files in the core KVM.
00032  *=======================================================================*/
00033 
00034 #ifndef _JAR_INT_H_
00035 #define _JAR_INT_H_
00036 
00037 /*
00038  * indicates whether JAR inflater uses standard i/o during decompression
00039  */
00040 #define JAR_INFLATER_USES_STDIO 1
00041 
00042 /*
00043  * The HuffmanCodeTable structure contains the dynamic huffman codes for
00044  * the Code Length Codes or the Distance Codes. The structure is
00045  * dynamically allocated. We just allocate enough space to contain all
00046  * possible codes.
00047  */
00048 
00049 typedef struct HuffmanCodeTableHeader {
00050     unsigned short quickBits;    /* quick bit size */
00051     unsigned short maxCodeLen;    /* Max number of bits in any code */
00052 } HuffmanCodeTableHeader;
00053 
00054 /* If this bit is set in a huffman entry, it means that this is not
00055  * really an entry, but a pointer into the long codes table.
00056  * The remaining 15 bits is the offset (in bytes) from the table header
00057  * to first "long" entry representing this item.
00058  */
00059 
00060 #define HUFFINFO_LONG_MASK 0x8000 /*  high bit set */
00061 
00062 #define MAX_QUICK_CXD  6
00063 #define MAX_QUICK_LXL  9
00064 
00065 /* For debugging, the following can be set to the name of a file (in quotes).
00066  * If we are decompressing something that is the exact same length as that
00067  * file, this will check to make sure that the decompressed bytes look
00068  * exactly the same as the bytes in the specified file.
00069  * java/lang/String.class is a particularly useful file to try.
00070  *
00071  */
00072 
00073 #ifndef inflateVerbose
00074 #define inflateVerbose 0
00075 #endif
00076 
00077 #if INCLUDEDEBUGCODE
00078     static void ziperr(const char * msg);
00079 #   define ASSERT(x) assert((x))
00080 #else
00081 #   define ziperr(msg)
00082 #   define ASSERT(x) (void)0
00083 #endif
00084 
00085 /* A normal sized huffman code table with a 9-bit quick bit */
00086 typedef struct HuffmanCodeTable { 
00087     struct HuffmanCodeTableHeader h;
00088     /* There are 1 << quickBit entries.  512 is just an example. 
00089      * For each entry:
00090      *     If the high bit is 0:
00091      *        Next 11 bits give the character
00092      *        Low   4 bits give the actual number of bits used
00093      *     If the high bit is 1:
00094      *        Next 15 bits give the offset (in bytes) from the header to 
00095      *        the first long entry dealing with this long code.
00096      */
00097     unsigned short entries[512];
00098 } HuffmanCodeTable;
00099 
00100 /* A small sized huffman code table with a 9-bit quick bit.  We have
00101  * this so that we can initialize fixedHuffmanDistanceTable in jartables.h
00102  */
00103 typedef struct shortHuffmanCodeTable { 
00104     struct HuffmanCodeTableHeader h;
00105     unsigned short entries[32];
00106 } shortHuffmanCodeTable;
00107 
00108 typedef struct inflaterState {
00109     /* The input stream */
00110     JarCompressedType inFile;
00111 
00112     int inRemaining;        /* Number of bytes left that we can read */
00113     unsigned int inDataSize;    /* Number of good bits in inData */
00114     unsigned long inData;    /* Low inDataSize bits are from stream.
00115                  * High unused bits must be zero 
00116                  */
00117     /* The output stream */
00118     unsigned char *out;        /* Current output position */
00119     unsigned char *outStart;    /* Start and end of output buffer */
00120     unsigned char *outEnd;
00121 #ifdef JAR_DEBUG_FILE  
00122     unsigned char *jarDebugBytes;
00123 #endif
00124 } inflaterState;
00125 
00126 /*=========================================================================
00127  * Macros used internally
00128  *=======================================================================*/
00129 
00130 /* Call this macro to make sure that we have at least "j" bits of
00131  * input available
00132  */
00133 
00134 #define NEEDBITS(j) {  \
00135       while (inDataSize < (j)) {                                \
00136            inData |= ((unsigned long)NEXTBYTE) << inDataSize; \
00137        inRemaining--; inDataSize += 8;                      \
00138       }                                     \
00139       ASSERT(inDataSize <= 32);                     \
00140 }
00141 
00142 /* Return (without consuming) the next "j" bits of the input */
00143 #define NEXTBITS(j) \
00144        (ASSERT((j) <= inDataSize), inData & ((1 << (j)) - 1))
00145 
00146 /* Consume (quietly) "j" bits of input, and make them no longer available
00147  * to the user
00148  */
00149 #define DUMPBITS(j) {                                          \
00150        ASSERT((j) <= inDataSize);                       \
00151        inData >>= (j);                                    \
00152        inDataSize -= (j);                                 \
00153     }  
00154 
00155 /* Read bits from the input stream and decode it using the specified
00156  * table.  The resulting decoded character is placed into "result".
00157  * If there is a problem, we goto "errorLabel"
00158  *
00159  * For speed, we assume that quickBits = table->h.quickBits and that
00160  * it has been cached into a variable.
00161  */
00162 
00163 #define GET_HUFFMAN_ENTRY(table, quickBits, result, errorLabel) { \
00164     unsigned int huff = table->entries[NEXTBITS(quickBits)];      \
00165     if (huff & HUFFINFO_LONG_MASK) {                   \
00166         long delta = (huff & ~HUFFINFO_LONG_MASK);                \
00167         unsigned short *table2 = (unsigned short *)((char *)table + delta); \
00168     huff = table2[NEXTBITS(table->h.maxCodeLen) >> quickBits]; \
00169     }                                                              \
00170     if (huff == 0) { goto errorLabel; }                   \
00171     DUMPBITS(huff & 0xF);                          \
00172     result = huff >> 4;                                            \
00173     }
00174 
00175 #if JAR_INFLATER_USES_STDIO
00176 #   define LOAD_INFILE_IF_NECESSARY
00177 #   define STORE_INFILE_IF_NECESSARY
00178 #   define INITIALIZE_INFILE_IF_NECESSARY  = state->inFile
00179 #   define NEXTBYTE                        fgetc(inFile)
00180 #   define COPY_N_BYTES(buffer, n)         (fread(buffer, 1, n, inFile) == n)
00181 
00182 #else 
00183 #   define LOAD_INFILE_IF_NECESSARY        inFile = state->inFile;
00184 #   define STORE_INFILE_IF_NECESSARY       state->inFile = inFile;
00185 #   define INITIALIZE_INFILE_IF_NECESSARY  
00186 #   define NEXTBYTE                        (*inFile++)
00187 #   define COPY_N_BYTES(buffer, n)         \
00188                            (memcpy(buffer, inFile, n), inFile += n, TRUE)
00189 #endif
00190 
00191 /* Copy values from the inflaterState structure to local variables */
00192 #define LOAD_IN                       \
00193     LOAD_INFILE_IF_NECESSARY          \
00194     inData = state->inData;           \
00195     inDataSize = state->inDataSize;   \
00196     inRemaining = state->inRemaining; 
00197 
00198 /* Copy values from local variables back to the inflaterState structure */
00199 #define STORE_IN                      \
00200     STORE_INFILE_IF_NECESSARY         \
00201     state->inData = inData;           \
00202     state->inDataSize = inDataSize;   \
00203     state->inRemaining = inRemaining; 
00204 
00205 #define DECLARE_IN_VARIABLES \
00206     register JarCompressedType inFile INITIALIZE_INFILE_IF_NECESSARY; \
00207     register unsigned long inData;         \
00208     register unsigned int inDataSize;      \
00209     register long inRemaining;             \
00210 
00211 #define LOAD_OUT out = state->out;
00212 
00213 #define STORE_OUT state->out = out;
00214 
00215 #define DECLARE_OUT_VARIABLES \
00216     register unsigned char *out;
00217 
00218 #endif /* JAR_INT_H_ */
Generated on Mon Mar 14 2016 15:25:28 for @PACKAGE_NAME@ by  1.7.1
